specs.torque_sequence: Start from center bolts and work outward in a spiral pattern
specs.notes: TTY bolts must be replaced. Do not reuse. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten from center outward in a spiral pattern
specs.notes: Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Use anti-seize on studs to prevent galling. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten in a spiral pattern from center outward
specs.notes: Do not overtighten aluminum oil pan. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Replace gasket. Do not overtighten. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
TTY bolts must be replaced. Check main bearing clearances. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten each rod bearing cap evenly
specs.notes: TTY bolts must be replaced. Check rod bearing clearances. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Use thin coat of anti-seize. Do not overtighten aluminum heads. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Use medium strength thread locker. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten evenly in a cross pattern
specs.notes: Replace gaskets and seals. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Always retorque after 25-50 miles. Check periodically. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Use medium strength thread locker. Critical safety component. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.

specs.lubrication: Special brake grease on slide pins
specs.notes: Use only brake-specific grease. Check slide pin movement. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Always replace copper washers. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten nut while holding bolt head
specs.notes: Critical suspension component. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Install new cotter pin. Check alignment after replacement. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten with suspension at ride height
specs.notes: Must torque at ride height to prevent bushing damage. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.

specs.torque_sequence: Hold strut shaft while tightening nut
specs.notes: Use strut shaft holding tool. Check alignment after service. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.

specs.torque_sequence: Hold ball joint stud while tightening
specs.notes: Hold stud with hex key to prevent spinning. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Use medium strength thread locker. Critical component. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Install new cotter pin or stake nut. Vehicle must be on ground for final torque. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten in a spiral pattern from center outward
specs.notes: Do not overtighten aluminum pan. Replace gasket or RTV. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Replace O-ring seal. Do not overtighten aluminum case. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Use medium strength thread locker. Mark driveshaft position before removal. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Ensure proper alignment during installation. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.
Replace crush washer. Do not overtighten aluminum oil pan. Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application.

specs.torque_sequence: Center outward in crossing pattern, 4 passes total
specs.notes: TTY bolts - replace with new bolts. Do not reuse. Follow exact sequence and stages.
Use crossing pattern starting from center bolts
Apply anti-seize to prevent seizure in aluminum head
Do not over-tighten - aluminum oil pan threads can strip easily
Low torque spec - do not over-tighten to avoid gasket damage

specs.torque_sequence: Caps 1-5 in order, both bolts per cap before moving to next
specs.notes: TTY bolts - replace with new bolts. Critical engine component.

specs.torque_sequence: Both bolts alternately in small increments
specs.notes: TTY bolts - replace with new bolts. Critical rotating assembly component.
Do not over-tighten. Use thin layer of anti-seize on aluminum heads.
Use threadlocker. Flywheel must be held during installation.

specs.torque_sequence: Various lengths - follow service manual pattern
specs.notes: Multiple bolt lengths used - ensure correct bolt placement
Final torque with vehicle on ground. Check after 50-100 miles.
Critical safety component - verify torque specification
Use proper brake caliper grease on slide pins
Always use new copper washers. Do not over-tighten.
Ensure proper seating before final torque
Install new cotter pin. May need to tighten slightly for pin alignment.
Must be torqued with vehicle at normal ride height
Hold strut shaft with hex key to prevent rotation
Hold ball joint stud to prevent spinning
Verify with OEM service manual for this specific application
Use new nut. Stake or use new cotter pin as applicable.
Do not over-tighten - can distort pan and cause leaks
Replace drain plug washer. Do not over-tighten.
Use threadlocker. Mark driveshaft position before removal.
Multiple bolt lengths - ensure proper bolt placement
Replace drain plug gasket. Do not over-tighten aluminum oil pan.

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center bolts and work outward in a cross pattern
specs.notes: TTY bolts - replace all bolts. Final angle torque critical for proper clamping force.

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center and work outward in spiral pattern
specs.notes: Tighten in 2-3 steps to final torque value

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center bolts and work outward
specs.notes: Use high-temperature anti-seize on threads to prevent seizing

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten in cross pattern from center outward
specs.notes: Do not overtighten - aluminum pan threads strip easily

specs.torque_sequence: Start from center bolts and work outward
specs.notes: Tighten gradually in 2-3 steps to avoid warping cover

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten caps in sequence from center main outward
specs.lubrication: Light engine oil on threads and under bolt head
specs.notes: TTY bolts - replace if removed. Check crankshaft end play after installation.
TTY bolts - always replace. Verify bearing clearances before final torque.
Thread plugs in by hand first. Use anti-seize to prevent thread damage.

specs.lubrication: Clean and dry threads with thread locker
specs.notes: Use medium-strength thread locker. Hold crankshaft while tightening.
Various bolt lengths - ensure correct bolt placement
Re-torque after 50-100 miles. Check for proper wheel seating.

specs.lubrication: Clean and dry threads with thread locker
specs.notes: Use medium-strength thread locker. Critical safety component.

specs.lubrication: High-temperature brake grease on pin only
specs.notes: Clean pin and apply proper brake grease before installation
Replace copper washers. Do not overtighten - can damage sealing washers.
Ensure ball joint stud is properly seated before tightening
Install cotter pin after torquing. May need to tighten further to align pin hole.
Tighten with vehicle at normal ride height for proper bushing alignment
Hold strut rod with hex key while tightening nut
Hold ball stud with hex key while tightening nut

specs.lubrication: Clean and dry threads with thread locker
specs.notes: Use medium-strength thread locker. Tighten gradually in star pattern.
One-time use nut. Stake collar after installation to prevent loosening.

specs.torque_sequence: Tighten in cross pattern from center outward
specs.notes: Do not overtighten - can warp pan or strip threads
Replace drain plug O-ring with each service

specs.lubrication: Clean and dry threads with thread locker
specs.notes: Use medium-strength thread locker. Mark driveshaft position before removal.
Various bolt lengths - ensure correct bolt placement in proper holes
Replace drain plug gasket with each oil change. Do not overtighten.
